Off Calendar Apr-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLD RIVER CONTRACTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: CHRISTOPHER RUIZ, ESQUIRE (MENEKSHE CARDWELL & RUIZ, 714.543.0593) APPEARING FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLD RIVER CONTRACTORS. MICHAEL T. KENNEDY, ESQUIRE (KOLETSKY MANCINI FELDMAN & MORROW, 510.272.4460) AND JESSE SULLIVAN, ESQUIRE (KIDD CARR, 510.268.8600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT MARTIN PROPERTIES.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: granted. Neither the complaint nor the general contractor's cross-complaint alleged misconduct by this party. To the contrary, the only specific allegation in paragraph 9 of the complaint, page 4 lines 3-5, attributes the problem to "defective design, construction and/or selection of materials for the framing of the structure" as the cause for the interior wall and fixture cracking. These allegations do not implicate the drywall work of this cross-defendant. Neither the Bzieff Declaration nor the Kennedy Declaration are admissible to establish that there is "a corner bead pops" problem. Neither witness has provided a factual foundation for the contention made or the qualifications to give such an opinion. Cross-complainant's request to allow plaintiff to amend the complaint is denied for lack of standing to make the request and as contrary to CCP section 437c and for the further reason that any amendment appears to be barred by the 10 year statute of limitations.
